brief_justification: Wrong answer (No instead of Yes) but the do-calculus reasoning approach was formally sound — the error is assuming P(Jyka) must be low because P(Yupt)=11%, ignoring that Yupt→Jyka could have a strong positive effect making P(Jyka|Yupt=0) high too; Deep multi-level causal analysis with DAG manipulation, do-calculus, and marginal distribution reasoning; Some self-monitoring via Amplify/Suppress framework and noting the observation-vs-intervention distinction; Verified via do-calculus rules and checked the mutilated graph, but failed to verify the actual marginal P(Jyka) computation; Presents conclusion as fairly certain without acknowledging that P(Jyka) depends on unknown conditional probabilities; Mentions observation vs intervention distinction but doesn't consider that P(Jyka) could be high even with low P(Yupt); Full derivation chain from DAG to mutilated graph to do-calculus identity, clearly traceable
